steps.title: Identify the condition that enables herding
steps.content: Herding occurs when peer imitation drives convergence on the same policy choice. This requires nations to have freedom to independently set policy; if an external body determines policy, herding cannot occur.

steps.title: Match the condition to the regional difference
steps.content:
C directly explains the discrepancy: African countries had their policies determined by international unions, while Asian countries set their own—the structural difference that accounts for why peer imitation could succeed in one region but fail in the other.

The other choices describe reserve characteristics or crisis impacts rather than explaining this difference.

That confirms C is correct.
